judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After initially identifying all NYSE Arca ETFs, the agent switched to a heuristic approach by generating a limited "candidate" set via keyword-matching on company descriptions (execute_python var_function-call-5714173529954912928) and only tested those tickers for MAX("Adj Close") > 200 (e.g., query_db calls returning BOIL, BZQ, COM, DUST, etc.), thereby omitting many qualifying ETFs and undercounting the total.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After initially identifying 1,435 NYSE Arca ETFs with data, the agent narrowed to a heuristic subset by filtering "Company Description" for leveraged/inverse keywords (execute_python result var_function-call-5008921188294955437) and only queried those batches, returning tickers instead of full names and missing many valid ETFs, so the plan did not comprehensively evaluate all ETFs as required.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent wrongly restricted candidates to leveraged-sounding ETFs by filtering "Company Description" with terms like 'Ultra', 'Bear', 'Bull', '2x', '3x' (query var_function-call-15366561466672223706) and then only executed a few partial UNION batches (e.g., var_function-call-13474247405689518817, -1835628469791321836, -2980356724729857977, -10754310361307560878), resulting in an incomplete set of 20 tickers rather than listing all qualifying NYSE Arca ETFs and the full count of 31.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: yes
FM3: no
FM4: no
@@

After correctly retrieving NYSE Arca ETFs and intersecting with available price tables, the agent applied a heuristic keyword filter to select only a subset of “candidate” ETFs (execute_python calls producing candidate lists) and then ran batch queries only on those (query_db calls returning a limited set of symbols), resulting in an incomplete list and count instead of exhaustively checking all Arca ETFs for 2015.
